mainEntity.name: How much does an N62 4.4 V8 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N62 4.4 V8 typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with used units from £1,500–£3,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW N62 4.4 V8 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N62 has a mixed reputation (3.5★). While it offers excellent performance and refinement, it is known for valve stem seal wear and cooling system leaks. Regular maintenance is essential, and some repairs are costly.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common N62 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Valve stem seal wear is the most common issue — causing blue smoke on startup and oil consumption. Cooling system leaks and alternator bracket oil leaks are also well-documented. Timing chain guide issues are less common than on the earlier M62.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the N62?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Land Rover application, the N62 4.4 V8 powered the Range Rover L322 from 2002 to 2005. The engine was also used across BMW's 5, 6, 7 Series and X5.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover N62 the same as BMW's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the N62B44 engine is mechanically identical across BMW and Land Rover fitments. Land Rover L322 applications used a slightly detuned version compared to some BMW applications, but the core engine, block, and internals are the same.

hero.subHeadline: The BMW N62 V8 is a smooth, advanced engine — but valve stem seals and coolant leaks are well‑documented weaknesses. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [ESTIMATED].

8 (Modern V8 complexity; valve seal replacement is labour‑intensive; specialised tools and knowledge required)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The N62 is a smooth, refined V8 — but valve stem seals and cooling system issues are costly — buy with documented service history and budget for maintenance.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers numerous BMW models including the 5 Series (E60/E61), 6 Series (E63/E64), 7 Series (E65/E66), and X5 (E53) → [BMW N62 page].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an N62 4.4 V8 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [INDUSTRY ESTIMATE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across BMW and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access and subframe design.

items.question: How much does an N62 4.4 V8 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned N62 4.4 V8 typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with used units from £1,500–£3,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

items.question: Is the BMW N62 4.4 V8 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The N62 has a mixed reputation (3.5★). While it offers excellent performance and refinement, it is known for valve stem seal wear (causing blue smoke on startup) and cooling system leaks. Regular maintenance is essential, and some repairs are costly.

items.question: What is the most common N62 failure?
items.answer: Valve stem seal wear is the most common issue — causing blue smoke on startup and oil consumption. Cooling system leaks (water pump, thermostat housing, and expansion tank) and alternator bracket oil leaks are also well‑documented. Timing chain guide issues are less common than on the earlier M62.

items.question: Which Land Rover models use the N62?
items.answer: In Land Rover application, the N62 4.4 V8 powered the Range Rover L322 from 2002 to 2005. The engine was also used across BMW's 5 Series (E60), 6 Series (E63/E64), 7 Series (E65/E66), and X5 (E53).

items.question: Is the Land Rover N62 the same as BMW's version?
items.answer: Yes — the N62B44 engine is mechanically identical across BMW and Land Rover fitments. Land Rover L322 applications used a slightly detuned version compared to some BMW applications, but the core engine, block, and internals are the same.
